text.heading: XXX UNIVERSITY, XXX, NIGERIA
REPORT OF THE AD-HOC COMMITTEE SET UP BY THE SENATE TO CONSIDER THE REPORT OF THE JOINT COMMITTEE OF COUNCIL AND SENATE SET UP BY THE COUNCIL TO REVIEW THE COMMITTE STRUCTURE OF THE UNIVERSITY
1. MEMBERSHIP
text.p: Professor - Chairman/Convenor
Professor O. - Member
Professor - Member
Mr. K. - Secretary
text.heading: 2. PREAMBLE
text.p: On 14th February, 2006, Council approved the proposal to set up a committee to review the committee system in the University in order to make it more effective and efficient. Council nominated three of its members to serve on the Joint Committee and requested Senate to nominate three if its members.
On the 19th March, 2006, Senate nominated three of its members to serve the Joint Committee.
The report of the Joint Committee was considered by Council on 21st February, 2007 and the new committee structure recommended was approved.
On the apparent realization of the fact that Senate's input was required on the matter the Joint Committee's report was forwarded to Senate.
On the 26th September, 2007, Senate decided to set up an Ad-Hoc Committee with the following terms of reference:
"To consider the committee structure as presented, determine its efficiency and effectiveness, make modifications where necessary and advise senate as appropriate".
text.heading: 3. METHOD OF WORK
text.p: The Senate Committee henceforth referred to as the committee held its inaugural meeting on Friday 25th April, 2008. Three other meetings were held on 14th May, 2008, and 1st June, 2008, and 1st June, 2008, and 4th August, 2008.

The Committee reflected on the terms of reference given to it, discussed the procedural issues that led to Council approving the joint Committee's report before seeking the input of the Senate and checked the enabling provision of the University laws to undertake the review of the Committee Structure.
The Committee then studied the report of the Joint Committee along with the existing Committee Structure as contained in the University Handbook. It thereafter developed its own structure.
text.heading: 4. GENERAL OBSERVATIONS
text.p: (a) As the Business Committee of the Senate earlier observed, Senate's input should have been sought on the Joint Committee's report before the council could consider it, let alone approve it.
The Chairman of the Senate may need to assure the Senate that the reviewed structure prepared by the Joint Committee and approved by Council without Senate's input is not being implemented already.

(b) In considering the report, the Ad-Hoc Committee noted that the legal backing for the establishment of Committees in the University was provided as section of the University main laws which states as follows:

"The Council, the Senate, the Board of any Faculty and any body of persons established by or in pursuance of the provisions of this law may respectively establish such Committee for the purposes of their respective functions as they may think fit".

(c) It was also noted that the Joint Committee did not indicate in its report, the justification for merger, modification, cancellation or retention of any Committee as recommended in the report. The reader was therefore left to do the tedious deductions.
text.heading: 5. CONCLUSIONS AND RECOMMENDATIONS
text.p: The Committee has diligently performed the duties assigned to it and came up with the findings, suggestions and recommendations contained in the attached papers. It is hoped that these recommendations would improve the dynamic Committee systems of our Great Institution.
Professor - Chairman
Professor O. - Member
Professor - Member
Mr. K. - (Secretary)
